title: Structured control flow: if/else, for/while loops, break/continue
goal: Extend M1.2 with structured selection (if/else) and iteration (for-range, while) constructs, lowered to SPIR-V structured control flow (OpLoopMerge, OpSelectionMerge, OpBranchConditional). Unlocks real algorithms inside kernels: reductions, prefix scans, any-thread iteration over work-per-thread.
scope_in: if / else if / else chains with bool condition
for i in range(start, end) { body } — inclusive-start, exclusive-end, u32 induction var
for i in range(start, end, step) { body } — with step (must be positive, compile-time constant for M1.3)
while condition { body } — bool condition, unbounded iteration
break; — exits innermost enclosing loop only
continue; — jumps to next iteration of innermost loop
Structured SPIR-V: OpLoopMerge(merge, continue_target, None) + OpSelectionMerge(merge) + OpBranchConditional; header-body-continue-merge block scheme per SPIR-V §2.11
HIR validation: break/continue outside a loop → error; break/continue in wrong scope (inside nested fn — but M1.3 has no nested fns) is N/A
HIR: for-range step must be a compile-time positive integer constant; variable step rejected as UnsupportedForStepInM1_3 or similar
Escape analysis for induction variable: it's immutable inside body (HIR rejects `i = 0;` inside for-body)
Example: examples/reduction.axc — per-workgroup sum of an SSBO via loop (single-threaded per workgroup for now; atomics + subgroup come M1.4)
Example: examples/vector_axpy.axc — non-parallel saxpy using a for loop (one thread does N multiplies/adds) — illustrates loop codegen without needing M1.4 subgroup
scope_out: Early return from kernel (M1.4; SPIR-V 1.3 structured return is tricky)
Iterator protocol / for x in container (never — AXIOM is explicit)
Match expression with control flow (later; match on ints per AXIOM parent)
Nested break/continue with labeled scopes (never; single-level only)
acceptance_criteria: cargo test --workspace passes (expected ≥300 tests after M1.3 additions; target floor 310)
spirv-val passes on emitted SPIR-V for examples/reduction.axc and examples/vector_axpy.axc (verified on CI where spirv-tools is installed)
HIR rejects break/continue outside a loop with UnsupportedBreakOutsideLoop / UnsupportedContinueOutsideLoop
HIR rejects for-step that is not a positive compile-time integer constant
HIR rejects assignment to for-loop induction variable inside body
Emitted SPIR-V contains OpLoopMerge for every loop and OpSelectionMerge for every if
Regression: all M0+M1.1+M1.2 tests still pass (252 baseline)
Determinism: compile same source twice → bytewise-identical SPIR-V
open_questions_for_architect: for-loop step: compile-time constant only, or runtime-variable? Latter is harder (no unroll), former simpler but less flexible. Recommend compile-time constant for M1.3; relax in M2.
Should break/continue be keywords (TokenKind::Break/Continue) that are already reserved in parser's M1_1_RESERVED_KEYWORDS? Check and remove from the reserved list.
Do we need a do-while or loop{} infinite-loop construct? Skip for M1.3; add if users request.

Structured control flow: SPIR-V §2.11 requires strict single-entry-single-exit headers. Any way to violate this from the surface language? E.g., `break; return;` — the second statement is unreachable. Treat as UnreachableCode warning or silent OK?
Induction variable scope: does `for i in range(0,10) { } let x = i;` reference i after the loop? Per usual semantics, no — i is loop-scoped. Spec it.
Loop header condition evaluated once or every iteration? Every iteration (standard). Spec it clearly.
